sections.paragraph:
ἐπισπουδάζω (s. σπουδάζω, σπουδή; Lucian, Pisc. 2; PHib 49, 3 [III b.c.]; PLille 3, 27; LXX) to be zealous in addition, be more zealous περί τινος for someone; but the text is prob. corrupt; ἐπί may be repeated from the preceding ἔτι Hs 2:6 v.l. (cp. the sound of the words in POxy 1172 ἔτι καὶ ἔτι ἐπισπουδάζει and s. MDibelius, Hdb. ad loc.). S. Whittaker mg.—DELG s.v. σπεύδω.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

PLille PLille = Papyrus grecs de Lille—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Whittaker Whittaker = MW.; s. H—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 380
